description.content:
Problem: suffix/root parsing logic is duplicated across suggestion, note, and voicing modules, making future chord support brittle.

--------------- [2026-04-06 13:43:51 +12:00] ----------
Validation: Partially valid. Most parsing is already centralized in parseChordName, but engine.js still adds small root/quality wrappers and keeps some chord-family logic separate.
Plan:
1. Audit the remaining parsing-related helpers in js/engine.js and decide which belong in js/chordNotes.js.
2. Replace wrapper-based root/quality extraction with direct use of parsed chord objects where possible.
3. Add focused parser/equivalence tests so future chord-support changes only need one parser update.

details.content:
Acceptance: one shared parser defines root and quality resolution, existing modules consume it, and supported chord types behave consistently everywhere.

Files: [js/engine.js](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/js/engine.js), [js/chordNotes.js](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/js/chordNotes.js), [js/chordVoicing.js](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/js/chordVoicing.js)

VALID but already partially done. chordNotes.js has getChordNotes (interval-based), and engine.js has its own getChordQuality/getChordRoot (regex-based). They solve the same problem differently. The duplication is real. Valid.

todo.title: Centralize chord parsing into one shared utility

description.content:
Problem: the current â€œfirstâ€ and â€œsecondâ€ inversions keep a bass root and do not behave like true inversions, which can make playback sound odd.

--------------- [2026-04-06 13:43:51 +12:00] ----------
Validation: Partially valid and partly outdated as written. The user-facing inversion options now generate true inversions via getInversionOptions, but progression smoothing still relies on buildVoicings, which keeps a doubled root-bass model and does not align with the newer inversion path.
Plan:
1. Rewrite this card around the remaining mismatch between buildVoicings and getInversionOptions.
2. Decide whether progression smoothing should keep root-bass voice-leading or switch to true inversion generation.
3. If true inversions should drive smoothing, update buildVoicings/chooseVoicing and cover the behaviour with playback tests.

details.content:
Acceptance: root position and inversions are generated correctly for triads and extended chords, and progression playback picks the closest valid voicing.

Files: [js/chordVoicing.js](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/js/chordVoicing.js), [js/playback.js](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/js/playback.js)

Valid. buildVoicings in chordVoicing.js keeps the bass root note present in all three voicings, so none of them behave as true inversions. The ticket is technically correct. Practical impact on sound is low but the code is misnamed and misleading.

todo.title: Correct inversion generation in chord voicing

description.content:
Problem: the app renders content into a, which is invalid HTML and weakens accessibility. 

--------------- [2026-04-06 13:43:51 +12:00] ----------
Validation: Still valid. index.html keeps #results as a ul, while renderSuggestions inserts a div.suggestions-wrapper into it.
Plan:
1. Keep #results list-based by rendering suggestion groups/items as li content, or change the host container to a div.
2. Update empty and error states to use the same semantics.
3. Refresh the suggestion-engine tests so the container structure is asserted.

details.content:
Acceptance: the suggestions area uses semantically correct markup throughout, including error and empty states.
Files: [index.html](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/index.html), [js/ui.js](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/js/ui.js)

Confirmed valid. index.html has a ul element with id="results" but ui.js renders a div element with class "suggestions-wrapper" into it. Putting div elements inside a ul is invalid HTML and weakens accessibility.

description.content:
Problem: the preview scripts assume a hardcoded local URL and donâ€™t actually start a server, so they only work in one specific environment.

--------------- [2026-04-06 13:43:51 +12:00] ----------
Validation: Still valid. open-browser.js still hardcodes http://127.0.0.1:5500/index.html, does not start a server, and package.json still points main at a missing index.js.
Plan:
1. Decide on one supported local preview path: auto-start a simple static server, or require a configurable URL.
2. Update the preview scripts to match that workflow instead of assuming Live Server on port 5500.
3. Fix package.json metadata and align README.md setup instructions with the scripts.

details.content:
Acceptance: preview commands either launch a local server automatically or clearly target a configurable URL that matches the documented workflow.

Files: [package.json](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/package.json), [open-browser.js](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/open-browser.js), [README.md](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/README.md)

Valid. The "main" field in package.json points to index.js which does not exist. The preview scripts open a browser to a hardcoded local URL without starting a server first, so they only work if you have already manually started one.

description.content:
Problem: runtime key generation can drift from intended theory without being noticed.

--------------- [2026-04-06 13:43:51 +12:00] ----------
Validation: Still valid. There are browser tests, but no direct regression coverage for generateAllKeys in js/theory.js.
Plan:
1. Add a small Node-based unit test file for js/theory.js.
2. Assert representative major, minor, and modal outputs: scale notes, diatonic chords, Roman numerals, relative key, and parallel key.
3. Add the test to the existing test script so theory regressions fail fast.

details.content:
Acceptance: tests assert the expected chords/functions for representative major and minor keys and catch incorrect relative/parallel relationships.

Files: [js/theory.js](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/js/theory.js), [js/dataLoader.js](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/js/dataLoader.js)

Valid. generateAllKeys in theory.js is a pure function. A test asserting that C Major produces the correct chords and Roman numeral functions costs almost nothing to write and would immediately catch any silent regression in key generation.

todo.title: Add regression tests for generated theory data

description.content:
Problem: the most error-prone logic is string parsing and enharmonic matching, but it has no direct tests. 

--------------- [2026-04-06 13:43:51 +12:00] ----------
Validation: Still valid. Current automated coverage is mostly app-level Playwright testing, not direct parsing/equivalence tests.
Plan:
1. Add isolated tests for parseChordName, normaliseRoot, parseProgression, and the chord-equivalence path.
2. Cover enharmonic roots, invalid tokens, slash chords, supported suffix families, and in-key matching edge cases.
3. Use these tests as the safety net before any further parser cleanup or refactor.

details.content:
Acceptance: tests cover canonicalization, enharmonic matches, invalid tokens, and progression parsing across major/minor contexts.

Files: [js/engine.js](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/js/engine.js), [js/chordNotes.js](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/js/chordNotes.js)

Valid and the most valuable testing work available. normaliseRoot, getChordNotes, parseProgression, and chordsEquivalent are all pure functions with no DOM or audio dependencies. They are the most error-prone parts of the codebase and ideal candidates for unit tests.

todo.title: Add unit tests for chord parsing and equivalence rules

description.content:
Acceptance: package metadata reflects the real app setup, and unused files are either removed or documented as reference-only.

Files: [package.json](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/package.json), [data/keys.json](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/data/keys.json), [js/dataLoader.js](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/js/dataLoader.js)

Confirmed valid. keys.json exists in the repository but dataLoader.js never loads it. All key data is generated programmatically from theory.js. The file is leftover from an earlier version of the app and should either be removed or documented as reference-only.

--------------- [2026-04-06 13:43:51 +12:00] ----------
Validation: Still valid. data/keys.json is still unused, and package.json still advertises a missing index.js as main.
Plan:
1. Decide whether data/keys.json is reference data worth keeping; if not, remove it.
2. If it stays, document clearly that runtime key data is generated from js/theory.js.
3. Correct package metadata so it reflects the real browser app entrypoints.

todo.title: Clean up stale project metadata and unused assets

description.content:
Problem: app.js still contains repeated play handlers and inline scale playback logic, despite the intended module boundaries.

--------------- [2026-04-06 13:43:51 +12:00] ----------
Validation: Still valid. The scale playback callback in refreshKeyUI still contains sequencing and timing logic that belongs with playback helpers.
Plan:
1. Extract the scale-note sequencing loop into js/playback.js.
2. Leave app.js responsible only for wiring callbacks and passing note arrays.
3. Add or update a focused integration test so scale playback still triggers the same audible order.

details.content:
Acceptance: app.js becomes mostly state and wiring, while audio/playback helpers live in dedicated modules with smaller callback surfaces.

Files: [js/app.js](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/js/app.js)

Valid. The inline scale playback callback in app.js is around 30 lines of playback logic covering octave climbing, MIDI note sequencing, and timing. This sits inside the orchestration layer and belongs in playback.js instead.

todo.title: Move playback-specific logic out of app orchestration.

description.content:
Problem: there are unused function parameters and helper functions that make the API surface noisier than it needs to be.

--------------- [2026-04-06 13:43:51 +12:00] ----------
Validation: Partially valid. The card does not point to strong examples now, but formatDisplayedChordLabel in js/ui.js appears unused and the overall API surface could be tightened.
Plan:
1. Do a quick unused-helper and unused-parameter audit before changing signatures.
2. Remove only helpers with no call sites and tighten signatures where arguments are demonstrably ignored.
3. Keep this as a low-priority cleanup unless the audit finds a larger maintenance cost.

details.content:
Acceptance: unused exports/params are removed, signatures are tightened, and no call sites pass ignored data.

Files: [js/ui.js](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/js/ui.js)

Partially valid. No specific examples were named in the ticket. Looking at the code, formatDisplayKeyName in ui.js appears to be defined but never called. Some render callbacks in app.js could also be tightened. Low priority.

todo.title: Remove dead parameters and unused helpers

description.content:
Problem: playback.js reimplements nearest-voicing selection instead of using the exported helper. 

--------------- [2026-04-06 13:43:51 +12:00] ----------
Validation: Still valid. playback.js still reimplements the closest-voicing loop instead of delegating to chooseVoicing.
Plan:
1. Replace the inline scoring loop in resolveChordVoicing with chooseVoicing.
2. Keep the default no-previous-voicing path behaviour unchanged.
3. Run playback and progression tests to confirm there is no audible regression.

details.content:
Acceptance: voicing choice lives in one place only, playback delegates to it, and behavior stays identical under tests.

Files: [js/chordVoicing.js](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/js/chordVoicing.js), [js/playback.js](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/js/playback.js)

Confirmed valid, and this was introduced during the recent refactor. playback.js reimplements the "pick closest voicing" loop inline inside playSoundChord, rather than calling chooseVoicing which already exists and is exported from chordVoicing.js. Straightforward to fix.

description.content:
Problem: the app creates a native AudioContext in one module and uses Toneâ€™s context in another, which adds confusion and risk around browser autoplay behavior. 

--------------- [2026-04-06 13:43:51 +12:00] ----------
Validation: Partially valid and partly outdated as written. playback.js no longer creates a native AudioContext, but synth.js still uses Tone for sampler playback and a separate native context for the metronome.
Plan:
1. Rewrite this card to focus on the remaining split between sampler and metronome context management.
2. Decide whether the metronome should move onto Tone's context/transport or remain native with clearer ownership.
3. Consolidate readiness and status reporting so one initialization path covers both systems.

details.content:
Problem: the app creates a native AudioContext in one module and uses Toneâ€™s context in another, which adds confusion and risk around browser autoplay behavior. Acceptance: there is one clear audio initialization path, one source of truth for readiness, and playback modules use it consistently.

Files: [js/playback.js](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/js/playback.js), [js/synth.js](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/js/synth.js), [js/app.js](h:/- ! Creations/Programming/Chord-Supporting-Helper-Application/js/app.js)

Confirmed valid. playback.js creates a native window.AudioContext object. synth.js uses Tone.js's own audio context via Tone.start(). These are two separate audio contexts running alongside each other. The native one in playback.js is never actually used for playback and is dead weight that adds confusion around browser autoplay policy handling.
